metadata.title:
MAY ADJUST


THE FRANKLIN


SCHOOL DEAL

School Board to Hold Out


for Full Value.

pages.raw_text:
There are indications today that
the controversy as to the value of
the Franklin school property,
which is sought by the Milwaukee
railroad, will be adjusted in a man.
ner that will result In the people
getting all that the property is
worth.


z. Stewart, county treasurer and
member of the school board,
says that no notice of the Mil
waukee's condemnation suit has
yet been filed on the board, and
that when notice is received the
school board will employ counsel
and contend that the full value of
the ground be paid.


r. ID. Sanders, the wholesale
grocer. who made the offer of
s150,000 for the site, after the
school board had tentatively
agreed to sell 1t to the Milwaukee
for 100000. said today:


II understand that the officials
of the Milwaukee have said that
they are willing to pay what the
property Is worth, which Is all that
/ am after. do not want the
ground. but rather than see the
property Of the people sacrificed.
| made the offer. l am glad to
know that the school board pro-
poses to contest the condemnation
case in court and strive to secure
the full value of the property.
That is all that any citizen can
ask"


It Is. understood that the school
board and the Milwaukee is trying
to get together on basis of $780,
900 for the ground and the value
of the building. which is said to
be worth $4,000 to $4,000 alone
for the salvage involved.
